OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.
Here is the required structure example (values are placeholders):

My background as a startup founder teaches me that clarity and structure matter; I approach the discussion with a concise agenda: explain the science, address the fears, and then tie it back to the core of why we care about our children and community. For instance, my daughter’s school will be better protected if we are vaccinated, which resonates with the same protective instincts that made me launch a health‑tech platform. I also use the fact that my religious community places a high premium on stewardship of the body to lighten the tone. I know that people will test me, so I keep my tone firm and unapologetic, refusing to be sidetracked by misinformation. response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.

persona.time_onsocialmedia: You spend roughly a few times weekly on social media

response_parsed.opinion: I find that when I sit down with friends or family to talk about vaccination, the most powerful tool is to frame the conversation around shared values rather than facts alone. My background as a startup founder teaches me that clarity and structure matter; I approach the discussion with a concise agenda: explain the science, address the fears, and then tie it back to the core of why we care about our children and community. For instance, my daughter’s school will be better protected if we are vaccinated, which resonates with the same protective instincts that made me launch a health‑tech platform. I also use the fact that my religious community places a high premium on stewardship of the body to lighten the tone. I know that people will test me, so I keep my tone firm and unapologetic, refusing to be sidetracked by misinformation. I lean on reliable sources—WHO, the Italian Ministry of Health, peer‑reviewed journals—and I share links in a way that feels supportive, not preachy. When someone brings up conspiracy theories, I calmly ask them where they read that, and I point them to evidence, showing that the benefit–risk ratio is overwhelmingly in favor of vaccination. If the conversation turns hostile, I steer it back to empathy: “I understand why you’re worried, but let’s look at the data.” I also set boundaries: if the other person refuses to listen, I politely disengage, because persistence can backfire. Finally, I practice self‑care; I know my own depressive episodes can color my perception, so I check in with a therapist and remind myself that respectful dialogue is a win for everyone.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: 1. Added citations. 6. Listed used persona fields. 7. Ensured single-line JSON output.
